 {"id":"wa-n8cd","title":"Robot workflow subcommands: unit + E2E tests with detailed logging","description":"# Robot workflow subcommands testing

## Purpose
Comprehensive testing for the harmonized workflow subcommand structure:
- `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane>` (syntax change from wa-guzr)
- `wa robot workflow list` (implementation in wa-35l)
- `wa robot workflow status <id>` (implementation in wa-7dd)
- `wa robot workflow abort <id>` (implementation in wa-55y)
- `wa robot events --unhandled` (alias from wa-st8m)

## Unit Tests

### CLI Parsing Tests (Already Implemented)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_run_subcommand_parsing() {
    // Old syntax should fail
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_err(), "Implicit run should be rejected");
    
    // New syntax should work
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "run", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_unhandled_flag_alias() {
    // Both flags should parse to same value
    let result1 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled"]);
    let result2 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled-only"]);
    assert_eq!(result1.unhandled_only, result2.unhandled_only);
}
```

### JSON Schema Validation Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_list_json_schema() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list"]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-list.json");
    assert!(output["data"]["workflows"].is_array());
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_status_json_schema() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-status.json");
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_abort_json_schema() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-abort.json");
}
```

### Error Code Stability Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_not_found_error() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", "nonexistent"]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND");
}
```

## E2E Tests

### Scenario: Full Workflow Lifecycle
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_lifecycle.sh
set -euo pipefail
LOG="$ARTIFACT_DIR/workflow_lifecycle.log"
log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== Workflow Lifecycle E2E ==="

# 1. List workflows
log "Listing workflows..."
LIST=$(wa robot workflow list)
log "List: $LIST"
echo "$LIST" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: list"; exit 1; }

# 2. Run a workflow (dry-run)
log "Starting workflow (dry-run)..."
RUN=$(wa robot workflow run handle_compaction 0 --dry-run || true)
log "Run: $RUN"

# 3. Check status (if execution ID available)
EXEC_ID=$(echo "$RUN" | jq -r '.data.execution_id // empty')
if [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ]; then
    log "Checking status for $EXEC_ID..."
    STATUS=$(wa robot workflow status "$EXEC_ID")
    log "Status: $STATUS"
fi

log "=== PASS: workflow_lifecycle ==="
```

### Scenario: Events Unhandled Flag
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_events_unhandled.sh
log "Testing --unhandled alias..."
O1=$(wa robot events --unhandled)
O2=$(wa robot events --unhandled-only)
echo "$O1" | jq -e '.ok' && echo "$O2" | jq -e '.ok' || exit 1
log "=== PASS: events_unhandled ==="
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamp every action
- Log raw JSON output for debugging
- Dump full output + stack trace on failure
- Summary: PASS/FAIL, elapsed_ms, key assertions

## Dependencies
- wa-35l: workflow list implementation
- wa-7dd: workflow status implementation
- wa-55y: workflow abort implementation
- wa-nu4.1.1: workflow engine core

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI parsing tests pass
- [ ] JSON schema validation tests pass
- [ ] Error code stability tests pass
- [ ] E2E workflow lifecycle test passes
- [ ] E2E events unhandled test passes
- [ ] Detailed logs in artifact directory","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:57.307902845Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.197377-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:57:40.957155542Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-n8cd","depends_on_id":"wa-35l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-n8cd","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-n8cd","depends_on_id":"wa-55y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-n8cd","depends_on_id":"wa-7dd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-n8cd","depends_on_id":"wa-hfis","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-n8cd","depends_on_id":"wa-lak7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-n9cp","title":"Content-addressable output dedup — deduplicate repetitive agent output","description":"## Goal
Implement content-addressable deduplication for captured pane output, exploiting the high repetition in AI agent terminal output to dramatically reduce storage.

## Background & Motivation
AI agent output is EXTREMELY repetitive. Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini all produce:
- Repeated status lines ("Thinking...", "Reading file...", progress bars)
- Repeated prompt templates (the entire system prompt appears in many sessions)
- Identical error messages across multiple panes
- Token-by-token streaming that creates many near-identical output segments

wa's storage already has a content_hash column on the output_segments table (storage.rs). This bead fully exploits it by:
1. Hashing each output segment's content (at the per-capture-cycle granularity — one segment per capture)
2. Storing unique content in a separate content_store table
3. Referencing by hash in output_segments (dedup at storage level)
4. Supporting cross-pane dedup (same content in multiple panes stored once)

## Dedup Granularity
Deduplication operates at the **output_segment** level (one segment = one capture cycle's delta for one pane). This is the natural unit because:
- It's the atomic unit of storage in output_segments
- The content_hash column already exists
- It captures meaningful chunks (not too small like per-line, not too large like per-session)
- Cross-pane dedup works well: agents running similar tasks produce identical segment-sized outputs

Line-level dedup was considered but rejected: the overhead of hashing every line exceeds the savings, and the content_hash column is already segment-level.

## Technical Design

### Schema Addition (Migration v20 → v21)
```sql
-- Content store: unique content blocks
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS content_store (
    content_hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    content BLOB NOT NULL,
    byte_size INTEGER NOT NULL,
    ref_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    first_seen INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- epoch_ms
    last_seen INTEGER NOT NULL    -- epoch_ms
);

-- Modify output_segments to reference content_store
-- output_segments.content becomes NULL when deduped
-- output_segments.content_hash references content_store.content_hash

-- Index for GC (find orphaned content)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_content_store_ref_count
    ON content_store(ref_count) WHERE ref_count = 0;
```

Note: This schema change is shared with bd-nz6 (snapshot storage tables). The migration should be coordinated — both features add to schema v21.

### Dedup Engine
```rust
// Location: crates/wa-core/src/storage.rs (extend existing)

pub async fn store_output_segment(&self, pane_id: PaneId, content: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
    let hash = blake3::hash(content).to_hex().to_string();

    // Upsert into content_store (dedup automatically via ON CONFLICT)
    self.db.execute(
        "INSERT INTO content_store (content_hash, content, byte_size, ref_count, first_seen, last_seen)
         VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, 1, ?4, ?4)
         ON CONFLICT(content_hash) DO UPDATE SET
             ref_count = ref_count + 1,
             last_seen = ?4",
        params![hash, content, content.len(), epoch_ms()],
    )?;

    // Store segment with hash reference, no inline content
    self.db.execute(
        "INSERT INTO output_segments (pane_id, content_hash, captured_at)
         VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)",
        params![pane_id, hash, epoch_ms()],
    )?;

    Ok(())
}

// Read content via hash lookup
pub fn get_segment_content(&self, segment_id: i64) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
    self.db.query_row(
        "SELECT cs.content FROM output_segments os
         JOIN content_store cs ON os.content_hash = cs.content_hash
         WHERE os.id = ?1",
        params![segment_id],
        |row| row.get(0),
    )
}
```

### Migration Strategy
For existing databases with inline content in output_segments:
```rust
pub fn migrate_to_dedup(&self) -> Result<MigrationReport> {
    // 1. Create content_store table
    // 2. For each unique content_hash in output_segments:
    //    INSERT INTO content_store with ref_count = COUNT(*)
    // 3. Set output_segments.content = NULL for deduped rows
    // 4. VACUUM to reclaim space
}
```

### Dedup Metrics
```rust
pub async fn dedup_stats(&self) -> Result<DedupStats> {
    let stats = self.db.query_row(
        "SELECT
            (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM output_segments) as total_segments,
            (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM content_store) as unique_blocks,
            (SELECT SUM(ref_count) FROM content_store) as total_refs,
            (SELECT SUM(byte_size) FROM content_store) as unique_bytes
         FROM (SELECT 1)",
        [],
        |row| Ok(DedupStats {
            total_segments: row.get(0)?,
            unique_blocks: row.get(1)?,
            total_references: row.get(2)?,
            unique_bytes: row.get(3)?,
        }),
    )?;
    Ok(stats)
}
```

## Existing Code References
- storage.rs (21K lines): output_segments table already has content_hash column
- ingest.rs: PaneCursor delta extraction (where content is captured and stored)
- wa-1nkt (cache GC): coordinates with dedup — GC decrements ref_count, removes orphaned content

## Expected Impact
- 40-60% storage reduction for typical agent workloads
- Cross-pane dedup is especially effective when running multiple agents on same project
- Faster cleanup (delete references, not content — content cleaned up by GC)
- FTS5 indexes only unique content (via content_store JOIN)

## Dependencies
None — standalone storage optimization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Identical content segments stored only once (verified via content_hash)
- Cross-pane deduplication works (same output in 5 panes = 1 content_store entry, ref_count=5)
- Reference counting correctly tracks usage
- GC integration: wa-1nkt's cleanup decrements ref_count and removes orphaned content
- FTS5 search still works correctly with deduped content
- Dedup stats available via wa robot API
- Migration for existing data (populate content_store from existing output_segments)
- blake3 used for hashing (fast, collision-resistant)

## Estimated Effort
3-4 hours implementation, 1-2 hours testing + migration

## Benchmark Requirements
- **criterion benchmarks** with target `benches/dedup_throughput.rs`:
  - `dedup_store_segment`: measure end-to-end dedup throughput (hash + upsert + insert). Target: >500 MB/s for content hashing and storage pipeline.
  - `dedup_lookup_by_hash`: measure content retrieval via hash JOIN. Target: <50us per lookup.
  - `dedup_stats_query`: measure dedup stats aggregation over 100K+ entries. Target: <10ms.

## Property-Based Testing (proptest)
- **Content-address invariant**: for any two byte slices `a` and `b`, if `a == b` then `blake3::hash(a) == blake3::hash(b)` (same content always produces same hash).
- **Dedup correctness**: storing N identical segments results in exactly 1 content_store entry with ref_count == N.
- **Roundtrip integrity**: for arbitrary byte content, `store_output_segment` followed by `get_segment_content` returns the original content byte-for-byte.
- **Ref-count monotonicity**: ref_count never decreases except via explicit GC decrement operations.

## Cross-References
- **wa-283h4.6** (Homomorphic stream hashing): provides complementary integrity verification for the content-addressed store; homomorphic hashes can verify dedup correctness incrementally without re-reading full content.
- **wa-3r5e** (Scrollback memory pressure mitigation): dedup reduces storage footprint, easing memory pressure; eviction policies in wa-3r5e must correctly decrement ref_counts when trimming deduped segments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:37:05.549734Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:08:43.534734-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:08:43.534734-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: DedupEngine with ContentStore trait, SHA-256 content hashing, ref-counted content blocks, GC, DedupStats with dedup_ratio/bytes_saved. 30 tests covering hashing, store/retrieve, cross-pane dedup, ref-counting, GC, property-based invariants.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-n9cp","depends_on_id":"wa-3cyp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:37:46.939878Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nau5","title":"MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs","description":"# Task: MCP tests (schema stability + parity)

## Goal
Prevent MCP from drifting away from robot mode.

## Test strategy
- Unit tests:
  - tool param validation
  - error codes
  - stable JSON schema snapshots
- Parity tests:
  - for a fixed fixture input (pane list/text/events), compare:
    - `wa robot ...` output
    - MCP tool output
  - they may differ in wrapper fields but must match in `data`

## Coverage requirements
- All tools listed in `wa-nu4.3.1.1` must have at least one schema test.
- Side-effect tools (`wa.send`, `wa.workflow_run`, `wa.reserve`, `wa.release`) must have policy‑deny tests.
- Reservation tools/resources must have parity tests vs robot outputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI runs MCP tests behind `--features mcp`.
- Adding a new MCP tool requires adding at least one schema/parity test.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Include at least one intentionally-different wrapper field to ensure parity tests compare the intended subset (not whole JSON).
  - Ensure schema tests fail if a required field is removed or an error code changes.


LABELS: area-mcp, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1.3: Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs ● P1
  → ○ bd-4vx.7.8: Robot mode tests: envelope stability, error codes, command outputs (synthetic) ● P2
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1.4: Implement MCP resources (wa://panes, events, accounts, workflows, rules) ● P2
","notes":"Started immediately after bd-nu4.3.1.4 completion. Initial deliverables landed in this session:
- Added MCP server registration tests asserting exact resources/templates with DB and without DB
- Added unhandled events resource template (wa://events/unhandled/{limit}) to satisfy optional-unhandled resource requirement
- Added base resource + template split for templated resources to preserve discoverable base URIs in FastMCP
Next on this bead: extend parity coverage between MCP resource payloads and robot/tool outputs for reservations/events/accounts/rules surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:22:27.775968512Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.184745-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T16:28:48.62223585Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nau5","depends_on_id":"wa-hfis","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nau5","depends_on_id":"wa-yi0l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nau5","depends_on_id":"wa-ou4o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
